name: datacommons-client
description: Work with Data Commons, a platform providing programmatic access to public statistical data from global sources. Use this skill when working with demographic data, economic indicators, health statistics, environmental data, or any public datasets available through Data Commons. Applicable for querying population statistics, GDP figures, unemployment rates, disease prevalence, geographic entity resolution, and exploring relationships between statistical entities.
qualityReason: Specific access to rich public datasets
capabilities: Query statistical observations by variable and entity
Resolve place names and coordinates to Data Commons IDs
Explore knowledge graph relationships between entities
Access time-series data for historical trends
Filter data by source for consistency
author: K-Dense-AI
securitySummary: This skill is a documentation wrapper for the Data Commons Python client library. All static findings are FALSE POSITIVES: the scanner misinterprets markdown code block delimiters as shell commands, API call examples as network threats, and legitimate documentation patterns as credential exposure. The skill enables read-only access to public statistical data with no code execution capabilities beyond package installation documentation.
valueStatement: Access global statistical data from Data Commons including demographics, economics, health, and environmental indicators. Query population figures, GDP, unemployment rates, and geographic relationships using Python client methods.
userTitle: Query public statistics from Data Commons
limitations: Requires Data Commons API key for datacommons.org access
Only supports place entities for resolution (countries, states, cities)
Cannot query non-place entities directly without DCID knowledge

useCases.title: Compare regional statistics
useCases.description: Query and compare population, income, and unemployment data across multiple states or countries.

useCases.title: Access historical trends
useCases.description: Retrieve time-series data for economic indicators, health statistics, or environmental measurements.

useCases.title: Build data-driven applications
useCases.description: Integrate public statistical data into applications using Python client library methods.

promptTemplates.title: Basic query
promptTemplates.prompt: Get the latest population for California, Texas, and New York using the Data Commons client.

promptTemplates.title: Time series
promptTemplates.prompt: Query the unemployment rate time series for the United States from 2010 to 2023.

promptTemplates.title: Geographic hierarchy
promptTemplates.prompt: Get median household income for all counties in California for year 2020.

promptTemplates.title: Multi-variable
promptTemplates.prompt: Compare population, median income, and median age across Florida, Georgia, and South Carolina.

bestPractices: Always resolve place names to DCIDs before querying to handle ambiguous names
Use entity expressions to query hierarchies efficiently (all counties in a state at once)
Cache DCID resolutions when querying the same entities repeatedly
antiPatterns: Hardcoding DCIDs instead of resolving names dynamically
Making individual queries for each entity instead of batch queries
Ignoring data source facets when consistency matters

faq.question: What is Data Commons?
faq.answer: Data Commons is a platform aggregating public statistical data from sources like census bureaus and health organizations into a unified knowledge graph.

faq.question: Do I need an API key?
faq.answer: Yes, for datacommons.org. Request one at apikeys.datacommons.org and set it via DC_API_KEY environment variable.

faq.question: What data types are available?
faq.answer: Population, income, unemployment, health statistics, environmental data, and geographic relationships for places worldwide.

faq.question: How do I find statistical variables?
faq.answer: Use fetch_available_statistical_variables() to check what data exists for an entity, or browse at datacommons.org/tools/statvar.

faq.question: Can I query historical data?
faq.answer: Yes, set date parameter to 'all' to retrieve complete time series for trend analysis.

faq.question: What are DCIDs?
faq.answer: Data Commons Identifiers are unique keys for entities. Use the Resolve API to convert place names to DCIDs.
